ground_truth_summary: Painting BY Hundertwasser, dated December 1965. 3 buildings: red circular tower (leftmost, pointy onion-dome roof); two yellow buildings (right) that are OPEN at top. Dark GROUND at bottom (NOT water). Blue horizon and sky. Onion domes in multiple colors. Embedded feminine face in LOWER-LEFT corner of RIGHTMOST building with very white skin and 2 RED dots on cheeks (signature motif). Blue teardrop shapes inside most windows.

strengths: Correctly identifies 3 buildings with red tower on left
Identifies onion domes and the recurring blue teardrop motif
Locates a face in the bottom right area of the yellow building
Notes thick brushstrokes and folk-art character
errors: HALLUCINATION: describes background/foreground as possibly 'a body of water' — the dark band at the bottom is GROUND, not water
WRONG DATE: reads signature as '2018' instead of 1965
Places face in 'bottom right corner' rather than lower-LEFT corner of the rightmost building
Claims one eye has a red dot — conflates cheek motif with eye; does not identify the 2 red cheek dots
Describes buildings as 'Russian-inspired' but does not name Hundertwasser
Describes floating frames but misses that the two right buildings are OPEN at the top
evaluations.notes: Solid structural description undermined by the water hallucination and a badly wrong date. Gets the teardrop motif right.

strengths: Best architectural description of the group — correctly distinguishes red cylindrical tower, cubic lime-green/yellow center, and pitched-roof right building
Correctly reads background as 'deep blue sky' / 'night sky' with NO water claim — avoids the major hallucination
Identifies face integrated into the right building
Identifies the recurring blue teardrop motif correctly
Calls the signature 'illegible' rather than fabricating a date — safest handling
errors: Does not identify Hundertwasser as the artist (common, but no attempt)
Face location given only as 'lower section of the pitched-roof building' — misses the specific lower-LEFT corner placement
Does not explicitly mention the 2 red dots on cheeks
Misses that the two right buildings are open at the top
evaluations.notes: Most accurate overall. Avoids the water hallucination, handles the signature cautiously, and correctly classifies the background as sky.

strengths: Correctly describes the ground as 'dark, almost black' — does NOT claim water (avoids hallucination)
Identifies the woman's face with pale skin, dark hair, and RED DOTS on cheeks (correctly identifies the signature motif)
Identifies onion domes and blue teardrops in windows
errors: WRONG DATE: reads signature as 'M. J. H. 1978' — incorrect year and fabricated initials
Says face is 'peeking out from behind the yellow building' — imprecise; actually embedded in lower-LEFT corner of rightmost building
Claims red dots on cheeks AND forehead — overcounts (only 2 dots on cheeks)
Fabricates 'floating geometric shapes in the sky including squares/rectangles' that are not prominent in the image
Does not identify Hundertwasser; calls style 'surreal/graphic'
evaluations.notes: Strong on the cheek-dots motif and ground description, but the fabricated 1978 date and imprecise face location drag it down.

strengths: Correctly identifies ground as 'black' — explicitly NOT water (avoids the major hallucination)
Correctly identifies face 'in lower right portion of the yellow building' with 'small red dots on the cheeks' — captures the signature motif
Identifies onion domes, blue teardrops, and arched vs rectangular windows
Safely describes signature as 'not legible' rather than inventing a date
Captures the surreal, folk-art quality and repeating teardrop theme
errors: Invents a title 'City of Windows and Tears' that is not in the image
Face placement 'lower right portion' is close but not specifically the lower-LEFT corner of the rightmost building
Does not identify Hundertwasser as the artist
Misses that the two right buildings are open at the top
Says 'no visible text' but a signature/date IS visible (just illegible)
evaluations.notes: Accurate on the load-bearing facts (ground not water, red cheek dots, teardrops, onion domes) and handles the signature cautiously. The invented title is a minor flourish, not a hallucination.

strengths: Correctly describes background transitioning to 'black at the bottom' — does NOT explicitly claim water (avoids the major hallucination)
Identifies face at 'bottom right corner of the yellow pyramid' with 'few red spots on the cheeks' — captures signature motif
Correctly identifies red tower with onion dome, lime-green/yellow center, yellow right building
Identifies the blue teardrop motif and arched windows on the red tower
Notes the texture and saturated color palette
errors: WRONG SIGNATURE: reads 'D. K.' followed by illegible script — fabricated initials; does not pick up 1965
Calls the style 'Op Art or folk art' — Op Art is incorrect; misses Hundertwasser
Face placement 'bottom right corner of yellow pyramid' is imprecise; should be lower-LEFT corner
Claims 'cluster of floating yellow squares and a strange bulbous shape' that are overstated
Misses that the two right buildings are open at the top
evaluations.notes: Competent description that avoids the water hallucination and correctly identifies the red cheek dots. Loses points for fabricated initials and the 'Op Art' mischaracterization.

ranking.reason: Cleanest description; correctly reads sky (not water) and handles signature cautiously
Correctly avoids water hallucination, captures red cheek dots and teardrop theme; minor title invention
Avoids water hallucination and gets cheek dots, but fabricates signature initials and mischaracterizes style as Op Art
Good structure but suggests water in background and badly misreads date as 2018
Gets red cheek dots right but fabricates a 1978 date and imprecisely locates the face

overall_notes: None of the five Q8 models identified the artist as Hundertwasser. The two strongest responses (gemma4-e4b, qwen3-vl-8b) both correctly avoided the 'water' hallucination and captured the 2-red-dots cheek motif. The three weaker responses either explicitly raised water (gemma4-12b) or fabricated date text (qwen3-vl-4b '1978', gemma4-12b '2018', qwen3.5-4b 'D. K.'). Notably, no model correctly identified that the two right buildings are OPEN at the top — a subtle detail in the ground truth that all five missed.
